_comment: Hand-curated overrides for irregular roots. Merged into the corpus-extracted lexicon by extract_lexicon.py. Captures phenomena the extractor can't infer: root allomorphy where the stem vowel itself changes, and lexically-marked aorist allomorphy.

overrides._note: de- (to say): becomes di- before -Hyor → diyor, not deyor
ye- (to eat): becomes yi- before -Hyor → yiyor, not yeyor

overrides._section: Monosyllabic verbs that take -Hr (high-vowel) aorist instead of the default -Ar. Canonical list from Turkish reference grammars.

Demonstrative and personal pronouns: insert pronominal -n- before case markers (bunu, şunu, onu, kendini). Modeled as a per-root flag pronominal_n that triggers the same n-insertion rule as POSS_3SG/POSS_3PL.

Personal pronouns. ben and sen are irregular in the dative: the stem vowel changes from -e to -a, so bana and sana surface — modeled via variants. They also have irregular GEN: benim, senim instead of benin, senin? Actually GEN is benim, senin which IS regular (-Hn). The GEN is regular. Only DAT is irregular: ben+e → bana, sen+e → sana.

overrides._note: I. ban variant only used before DAT: ban+a → bana
you (sg). san variant only used before DAT: san+a → sana

overrides._section: Reflexive pronoun: kendi 'self/own'. Takes possessive suffixes (kendim 'myself', kendin 'yourself', kendisi 'himself/herself/itself'). With possessive + case, the pronominal-n surfaces normally via the POSS_3SG/POSS_3PL n-insertion rule. Plain 'kendi' alone is the genitive/adjectival form 'one's own'.

overrides._note: self / one's own. With possessive: kendim, kendin, kendisi, kendimiz, kendiniz, kendileri
where (interrogative locative root; takes case: nerede, nereye, nereden)
each other (root form; takes possessive: birbirimiz 'each other (1pl)', birbiri 'each other (3sg)')

overrides._section: Plural demonstratives. These are bu/şu/o + lar with pronominal-n behavior preserved: 'onları' (acc) decomposes as o+PLUR+ACC with n-insertion. But the lexicalized forms bunlar/şunlar/onlar appear as their own headwords too; we leave them out and let the parser decompose.

Question particles mi/mı/mu/mü. In Turkish orthography these are written as separate words (Geldin mi? = 'Did you come?'). The form harmonizes to the previous stem's vowel: e/i → mi, a/ı → mı, o/u → mu, ö/ü → mü. We list all four allomorphs as separate lex entries with high frequency. Class is NOUN (UD treats it as PART; mapping to NOUN keeps the lexicon-loader simple while still parsing correctly).
Negative/non-existential particle 'değil' and existential 'var/yok'. These are extremely common but tend to be missed by extraction.

overrides._note: negation/copular not (Bu kitap değil = 'This is not a book')
existential 'there is'. Also a regular verb meaning 'to arrive'.
